content.full_text:
California NOW PAC                                                                                                ID # 820364
                                   926 J Street, Suite 323 Sacramento, CA 95814 (916) 442-3414
                                                                                                                    MAY 16 9 03 AM '90

May 14, 1990

Kevin Braaten-Moen
Fair Political Practices Commission
482 J Street, Suite 610
Sacramento, CA 95814

This memo is to confirm our phone conversation today in which you indicated that
the FPPC would have no problems with the following proposed slate mailer.

A group of candidates and propositions, all of whom have been endorsed by the
California NOW PAC, will be listed on a slate mailer to go to 300,000 women voters.
Each candidate or proposition will pay their proportion of the expenses directly to
the printer, mailing house, post office, etc. There will be no cost to the California
NOW PAC and no money will go through the California NOW PAC. The
California NOW PAC endorsement will be the unifying theme of the mailer. As
Chair of the California NOW PAC, I will sign a letter on the slate mailer, urging
support for the candidates and propositions included.

Part of the slate mailer will be a membership application for California NOW, Inc., a
501 (c) (4) non-profit organization giving the 300,000 women the option of joining
NOW. There will be no cost to California NOW, Inc. and the candidates and
propositions will not be making a financial contribution directly to California
NOW, Inc.

If you need any further details, please give me a call at 442-3414. I would appreciate
it if you would confirm our phone conversation in writing.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Linda Joplin
PAC Chair


"Never Another Season of Silence" - Susan B. Anthony
                                                                                                                              26

sections.question_synthetic: The sender seeks written confirmation that a proposed slate mailer arrangement, where endorsed candidates and propositions share printing and mailing costs directly with vendors (with no money flowing through the PAC itself), complies with FPPC regulations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/correspondence, not an FPPC advice letter response. No formal Q&A sections present. Document references a prior phone conversation with Kevin Braaten-Moen of FPPC and requests written confirmation. Sender's signature block appears damaged/unclear. This should be classified as incoming_request but document_type field options provided do not include that value, so correspondence is most appropriate classification.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The sender seeks written confirmation that a proposed slate mailer arrangement, where endorsed candidates and propositions share printing and mailing costs directly with vendors (with no money flowing through the PAC itself), complies with FPPC regulations.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming correspondence from Linda Poplin, Chair of California NOW PAC, requesting written confirmation from FPPC regarding a previously discussed phone conversation about a slate mailer arrangement where candidates and propositions would share direct costs with vendors while the PAC provides endorsement.
